panels.description: How many standard deviations latency sits from its own 6h normal. |z| > 3 (shaded) is a strong anomaly.
panels.title: Latency Anomaly Z-Score (per target)

panels.description: Gap between the fastest and slowest provider. A large spread = one path degrading while others are fine (path-specific, not a global outage).
panels.title: Provider Latency Spread (divergence)

panels.description: Root-cause classifier. 'All providers down' = your link/ISP. 'Single-provider issue' = that one provider only.
panels.title: Fault Scope — ISP/link vs single provider

panels.description: Live latency vs the historical median for this hour-of-week. Expected line appears once a few days of history exist.
panels.title: Latency vs Seasonal Baseline

panels.description: Actual / expected for this hour-of-week. 1.0 = normal for now; >1.5 = notably slower than this time usually is.
panels.title: Seasonal Deviation Ratio (per target)

panels.description: Times the whole internet (all providers at once) went down within the selected range.
panels.title: Total Internet Outages

panels.description: Duration of the most recent completed full outage.
panels.title: Last Outage Duration

panels.description: If > 0, the internet is down RIGHT NOW and has been for this many seconds.
panels.title: Current Outage (live)

panels.description: Cumulative count of outages per target over time (steps up at each outage).
panels.title: Outage Count per Target (cumulative)

panels.description: Latency to each hop on the way to the target. Hop 1 = your router; hop 2-3 = ISP first hops; beyond = upstream. A jump at a hop localizes the fault.
panels.title: Per-Hop Latency (path to target)

panels.description: Packet loss at each hop. Loss that starts at a hop and continues = a real problem there. Loss at one hop that does NOT propagate downstream is usually just that router de-prioritizing ICMP (harmless).
panels.title: Per-Hop Packet Loss

panels.description: Bufferbloat grade from latency increase under load. A=excellent, F=severe. Low grade = calls/games suffer when the link is busy, even if speed looks fine.
panels.title: Bufferbloat Grade

panels.description: Latency at idle vs while the link is saturated downloading/uploading. The gap is bufferbloat.
panels.title: Latency Under Load (bufferbloat)

panels.description: Measured throughput over time (Cloudflare speed endpoints).
panels.title: Throughput (Download / Upload Mbps)

panels.description: Most recent Ookla speedtest result. Runs every hour (LOADTEST_INTERVAL_SECONDS). Value persists until next run.
panels.title: Latest Speed

panels.description: Cumulative bytes downloaded across all speedtest runs. Persisted across container restarts. Updates once per hour.
panels.title: ↓ Total Downloaded

panels.description: Cumulative bytes uploaded across all speedtest runs. Persisted across container restarts. Updates once per hour.
panels.title: ↑ Total Uploaded
